heading: Other definitions and special rules

(e)(2)(B). substituted “section 174A(a) (relating to domestic research or experimental expenditures)” for “section 174(a) (relating to research and experimental expenditures)”.Pub. L. 119–212022—Subsec. (k). , added subsec. (k).Pub. L. 117–169, § 10101(a)(2)Subsec. (k)(1)(D). , amended subpar. (D) generally. Prior to amendment, text of subpar. (D) read as follows:Pub. L. 117–169, § 13904(a)“(i) .—Solely for purposes of determining whether a corporation is an applicable corporation under this paragraph, all adjusted financial statement income of persons treated as a single employer with such corporation under subsection (a) or (b) of section 52 (determined with the modifications described in clause (ii)) shall be treated as adjusted financial statement income of such corporation, and adjusted financial statement income of such corporation shall be determined without regard to paragraphs (2)(D)(i) and (11) of section 56A(c).In general“(ii) .—For purposes of this subparagraph—Modifications“(I) section 52(a) shall be applied by substituting ‘component members’ for ‘members’, and“(II) for purposes of applying section 52(b), the term ‘trade or business’ shall include any activity treated as a trade or business under paragraph (5) or (6) of section 469(c) (determined without regard to the phrase ‘To the extent provided in regulations’ in such paragraph (6)).“(iii) .—For purposes of this subparagraph, the term ‘component member’ has the meaning given such term by section 1563(b), except that the determination shall be made without regard to section 1563(b)(2).”Component memberSubsec. L. 105–34, § 1057(a)“(C) .—Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any domestic corporation if—Exception“(i) more than 50 percent of the stock of such domestic corporation (by vote and value) is owned by United States persons who are not members of an affiliated group (as defined in section 1504 of such Code) which includes such corporation,“(ii) all of the activities of such corporation are conducted in 1 foreign country with which the United States has an income tax treaty in effect and such treaty provides for the exchange of information between such foreign country and the United States,“(iii) all of the current earnings and profits of such corporation are distributed at least annually (other than current earnings and profits retained for normal maintenance or capital replacements or improvements of an existing business), and“(iv) all of such distributions by such corporation to United States persons are used by such persons in a trade or business conducted in the United States.”Subsec. children.heading: Alternative minimum tax foreign tax credit
children.label: a Alternative minimum tax foreign tax credit
A
children.text: the pre-credit tentative minimum tax were the tax against which such credit was taken for purposes of section 904 for the taxable year and all prior taxable years beginning after ,December 31, 1986

children.label: B
children.text: section 904 were applied on the basis of alternative minimum taxable income instead of taxable income, and

children.label: C
children.text: the determination of whether any income is high-taxed income for purposes of section 904(d)(2) were made on the basis of the applicable rate specified in section 55(b)(1) in lieu of the highest rate of tax specified in section 1.

children.heading: Pre-credit tentative minimum tax
children.label: 2 Pre-credit tentative minimum tax
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “pre-credit tentative minimum tax” means the amount determined under the first sentence of section 55(b)(1)(A).

children.heading: Election to use simplified section 904 limitation
children.label: 3 Election to use simplified section 904 limitation
i
children.text: subparagraph (B) of paragraph (1) shall not apply, and

children.label: I
children.text: the taxpayer’s taxable income (as determined for purposes of the regular tax) from sources without the United States (but not in excess of the taxpayer’s entire alternative minimum taxable income), bears to

children.label: II
children.text: the taxpayer’s entire alternative minimum taxable income for the taxable year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: An election under this paragraph may be made only for the taxpayer’s first taxable year which begins after , and for which the taxpayer claims an alternative minimum tax foreign tax credit.December 31, 1997

children.heading: Election revocable only with consent
children.label: ii Election revocable only with consent
children.text: An election under this paragraph, once made, shall apply to the taxable year for which made and all subsequent taxable years unless revoked with the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: Treatment of estates and trusts
children.label: c Treatment of estates and trusts
children.text: In the case of any estate or trust, the alternative minimum taxable income of such estate or trust and any beneficiary thereof shall be determined by applying part I of subchapter J with the adjustments provided in this part.

children.heading: Apportionment of differently treated items in case of certain entities
children.label: d Apportionment of differently treated items in case of certain entities

children.heading: Regulated investment companies and real estate investment trusts
children.label: A Regulated investment companies and real estate investment trusts
children.text: In the case of a regulated investment company to which part I of subchapter M applies or a real estate investment company to which part II of subchapter M applies, between such company or trust and shareholders and holders of beneficial interest in such company or trust.

children.heading: Common trust funds
children.label: B Common trust funds
children.text: In the case of a common trust fund (as defined in section 584(a)), pro rata among the participants of such fund.

children.heading: Differently treated items
children.label: 2 Differently treated items
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “differently treated item” means any item of tax preference or any other item which is treated differently for purposes of this part than for purposes of computing the regular tax.

children.heading: Optional 10-year writeoff of certain tax preferences
children.label: e Optional 10-year writeoff of certain tax preferences

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: For purposes of this title, any qualified expenditure to which an election under this paragraph applies shall be allowed as a deduction ratably over the 10-year period (3-year period in the case of circulation expenditures described in section 173) beginning with the taxable year in which such expenditure was made (or, in the case of a qualified expenditure described in paragraph (2)(C), over the 60-month period beginning with the month in which such expenditure was paid or incurred).

children.label: A
children.text: section 173 (relating to circulation expenditures),

children.label: B
children.text: section 174A(a) (relating to domestic research or experimental expenditures),

children.label: C
children.text: section 263(c) (relating to intangible drilling and development expenditures),

children.label: D
children.text: section 616(a) (relating to development expenditures), or

children.label: E
children.text: section 617(a) (relating to mining exploration expenditures).

children.heading: Other sections not applicable
children.label: 3 Other sections not applicable
children.text: Except as provided in this subsection, no deduction shall be allowed under any other section for any qualified expenditure to which an election under this subsection applies.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: An election may be made under paragraph (1) with respect to any portion of any qualified expenditure.

children.heading: Revocable only with consent
children.label: B Revocable only with consent
children.text: Any election under this subsection may be revoked only with the consent of the Secretary.

children.heading: Partners and shareholders of S corporations
children.label: C Partners and shareholders of S corporations
children.text: In the case of a partnership, any election under paragraph (1) shall be made separately by each partner with respect to the partner’s allocable share of any qualified expenditure. A similar rule shall apply in the case of an S corporation and its shareholders.

children.heading: Application of section 1254
children.label: A Application of section 1254
children.text: In the case of any disposition of property to which section 1254 applies (determined without regard to this section), any deduction under paragraph (1) with respect to amounts which are allocable to such property shall, for purposes of section 1254, be treated as a deduction allowable under section 263(c), 616(a), or 617(a), whichever is appropriate.

children.heading: Application of section 617(d)
children.label: B Application of section 617(d)
children.text: In the case of any disposition of mining property to which section 617(d) applies (determined without regard to this subsection), any deduction under paragraph (1) with respect to amounts which are allocable to such property shall, for purposes of section 617(d), be treated as a deduction allowable under section 617(a).

children.heading: Amounts to which election apply not treated as tax preference
children.label: 6 Amounts to which election apply not treated as tax preference
children.text: Any portion of any qualified expenditure to which an election under paragraph (1) applies shall not be treated as an item of tax preference under section 57(a) and section 56 shall not apply to such expenditure.

children.heading: Tax benefit rule
children.label: g Tax benefit rule
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe regulations under which differently treated items shall be properly adjusted where the tax treatment giving rise to such items will not result in the reduction of the taxpayer’s regular tax for the taxable year for which the item is taken into account or for any other taxable year.

children.heading: Coordination with certain limitations
children.label: h Coordination with certain limitations
children.text: The limitations of sections 704(d), 465, and 1366(d) (and such other provisions as may be specified in regulations) shall be applied for purposes of computing the alternative minimum taxable income of the taxpayer for the taxable year with the adjustments of sections 56, 57, and 58.

children.heading: Special rule for amounts treated as tax preference
children.label: i Special rule for amounts treated as tax preference
children.text: For purposes of this subtitle (other than this part), any amount shall not fail to be treated as wholly exempt from tax imposed by this subtitle solely by reason of being included in alternative minimum taxable income.

children.heading: Treatment of unearned income of minor children
children.label: j Treatment of unearned income of minor children
A
children.text: such child’s earned income (as defined in section 911(d)(2)) for the taxable year, plus

children.label: A
children.text: such dollar amount, and

children.label: B
children.text: the cost-of-living adjustment determined under section 1(f)(3) for the calendar year in which the taxable year begins, determined by substituting “1997” for “2016” in subparagraph (A)(ii) thereof.

children.label: i
children.text: are prior to such taxable year, and

children.label: ii
children.text: end after .December 31, 2021

children.heading: Average annual adjusted financial statement income test
children.label: B Average annual adjusted financial statement income test
i
children.text: a corporation meets the average annual adjusted financial statement income test for a taxable year if the average annual adjusted financial statement income of such corporation (determined without regard to section 56A(d)) for the 3-taxable-year period ending with such taxable year exceeds $1,000,000,000, and

children.label: I
children.text: the corporation meets the requirements of clause (i) for such taxable year (determined after the application of paragraph (2)), and

children.label: II
children.text: the average annual adjusted financial statement income of such corporation (determined without regard to the application of paragraph (2) and without regard to section 56A(d)) for the 3-taxable-year-period ending with such taxable year is $100,000,000 or more.

children.label: I
children.text: has a change in ownership, or

children.label: II
children.text: has a specified number (to be determined by the Secretary and which shall, as appropriate, take into account the facts and circumstances of the taxpayer) of consecutive taxable years, including the most recent taxable year, in which the corporation does not meet the average annual adjusted financial statement income test of subparagraph (B), and

children.label: ii
children.text: the Secretary determines that it would not be appropriate to continue to treat such corporation as an applicable corporation.

children.heading: Special rules for determining applicable corporation status
children.label: D Special rules for determining applicable corporation status
children.text: Solely for purposes of determining whether a corporation is an applicable corporation under this paragraph, all adjusted financial statement income of persons treated as a single employer with such corporation under subsection (a) or (b) of section 52 shall be treated as adjusted financial statement income of such corporation, and adjusted financial statement income of such corporation shall be determined without regard to paragraphs (2)(D)(i) and (11) of section 56A(c).

children.heading: Corporations in existence for less than 3 years
children.label: i Corporations in existence for less than 3 years
children.text: If the corporation was in existence for less than 3-taxable years, subparagraph (B) shall be applied on the basis of the period during which such corporation was in existence.

children.heading: Short taxable years
children.label: ii Short taxable years
children.text: Adjusted financial statement income for any taxable year of less than 12 months shall be annualized by multiplying the adjusted financial statement income for the short period by 12 and dividing the result by the number of months in the short period.

children.heading: Treatment of predecessors
children.label: iii Treatment of predecessors
children.text: Any reference in this subparagraph to a corporation shall include a reference to any predecessor of such corporation.

children.heading: Special rule for foreign-parented multinational groups
children.label: 2 Special rule for foreign-parented multinational groups

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: If a corporation is a member of a foreign-parented multinational group for any taxable year, then, solely for purposes of determining whether such corporation meets the average annual adjusted financial statement income test under paragraph (1)(B)(ii)(I) for such taxable year, the adjusted financial statement income of such corporation for such taxable year shall include the adjusted financial statement income of all members of such group. Solely for purposes of this subparagraph, adjusted financial statement income shall be determined without regard to paragraphs (2)(D)(i), (3), (4), and (11) of section 56A(c).

children.label: i
children.text: at least one entity is a domestic corporation and another entity is a foreign corporation,

children.label: ii
children.text: such entities are included in the same applicable financial statement with respect to such year, and

children.label: I
children.text: the common parent of such entities is a foreign corporation, or

children.label: II
children.text: if there is no common parent, the entities are treated as having a common parent which is a foreign corporation under subparagraph (D).

children.heading: Foreign corporations engaged in a trade or business within the United States
children.label: C Foreign corporations engaged in a trade or business within the United States
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, if a foreign corporation is engaged in a trade or business within the United States, such trade or business shall be treated as a separate domestic corporation that is wholly owned by the foreign corporation.

children.label: i
children.text: the entities (if any) which are to be to be treated under subparagraph (B)(iii)(II) as having a common parent which is a foreign corporation,

children.label: ii
children.text: the entities to be included in a foreign-parented multinational group, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the common parent of a foreign-parented multinational group.

children.label: A
children.text: providing a simplified method for determining whether a corporation meets the requirements of paragraph (1), and’(B) addressing the application of this subsection to a corporation that experiences a change in ownership.1So in original.1

children.heading: Corporate AMT foreign tax credit
children.label: l Corporate AMT foreign tax credit
I
children.text: taken into account on the applicable financial statement of each controlled foreign corporation with respect to which the applicable corporation is a United States shareholder, and

children.label: II
children.text: paid or accrued (for Federal income tax purposes) by each such controlled foreign corporation, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the product of the amount of the adjustment under section 56A(c)(3) and the percentage specified in section 55(b)(2)(A)(i), and

children.label: i
children.text: taken into account on the applicable corporation’s applicable financial statement, and

children.label: ii
children.text: paid or accrued (for Federal income tax purposes) by the applicable corporation.

children.heading: Carryover of excess tax paid
children.label: 2 Carryover of excess tax paid
children.text: For any taxable year for which an applicable corporation chooses to have the benefits of subpart A of part III of subchapter N, the excess of the amount described in paragraph (1)(A)(i) over the amount described in paragraph (1)(A)(ii) shall increase the amount described in paragraph (1)(A)(i) in any of the first 5 succeeding taxable years to the extent not taken into account in a prior taxable year.

children.heading: Regulations or other guidance
children.label: 3 Regulations or other guidance
children.text: The Secretary shall provide for such regulations or other guidance as is necessary to carry out the purposes of this subsection.
